goal: Exploration should feel dangerous immediately without time-based difficulty ramping. Daytime wilderness pressure should come primarily from daylight-safe modded mobs, while extra difficulty comes from Atlas region/ring identity instead of CRD, day count, or elapsed time.

majruszsdifficulty.time_or_crd_difficulty_scaling: disabled; all is_scaled_by_crd flags are false and crd_penalty is 0.0 for all stages

incontrol_hostile_spawner.uses_biome_native_monsters: cave-only; removed from open-sky daytime injection to avoid burning undead under sun
surface_rules: daylight-safe modded wilderness predators, raiders, casters, and elites
incontrol_hostile_spawner.explicit_open_sky_burning_undead_policy: Do not inject zombies, skeletons, strays, or other burning undead into open-sky daytime pools.
safety_controls: No dragons are explicitly injected as common spawns.
No major structure bosses are made common by spawner.json.
Surface rules avoid village structure tags.
Rules use maxhostile, maxlocal, and maxthis caps.

behavior: Eligible hostile mobs spawned near a player receive an Atlas region tag.
Regional profiles apply hidden strength, speed, or resistance effects according to region identity.
Far Atlas rings add a small extra wilderness edge without using elapsed time.

kept_controlled: Hearthlands still denies routine boss/dragon-tier namespaces.
Sunreach still denies Aquamirae as desert filler.
Open-sky daytime spawner rules do not inject burning vanilla undead.
Difficulty multipliers are now region/ring based, not time/day-count based.
Dragons and named major bosses are not explicitly injected as routine spawner mobs.
superseded_notes: The first density pass did not add spawner.json. The follow-up hostile pass now adds config/incontrol/spawner.json because natural spawn weights improved wildlife but did not create enough daytime enemies.
test_expectation: Daytime open land should show multiple visible modded threats, not mostly zombies and skeletons burning in the sun.
Night should remain dangerous from the normal monster stack plus the stronger flat baseline.
Caves should remain busy through biome-native monster reinforcement.
The difficulty should feel dangerous immediately instead of ramping because a chunk has been inhabited longer.
The same hostile mob should be noticeably harsher in diagonal frontier and outer regions than in Hearthlands.
Villages should be watched for pressure even though surface rules still avoid village structure tags.
